lesson: Soft product, a frosted suction line and near-zero superheat point to a stuck-closed expansion valve starving the evaporator — not overcharge. Check superheat.
symptoms: Poor cooling — product soft at barrel.
Suction line heavily frosted all the way to the compressor.
Product starts firm, then turns soft as serving continues.
White frost creeps along the suction pipe toward the compressor.
Barrel takes far too long to recover between portions.
The low-side line is buried under ice.
Compressor sounds normal but cannot hold serving temperature.

tests.error-log: Repeated refrigeration warnings — temperatures drifting out of range under load. No hard fault codes.
tests.temp-probe: Suction line at −20 °C — far colder than it should be — and superheat reading near zero.

flavour: The valve was frozen with indecision. Replaced.
authenticityNote: A stuck-closed expansion valve starves the evaporator and drives superheat to near-zero, frosting the suction line back to the compressor. The symptom pattern looks like overcharge until you check superheat.
